base_case: Bear/corrective (30–60 day horizon) — the melt-up thesis has failed; a confirmed institutional-distribution correction is underway from the 7,620 intraday ATH. Golden Cross regime remains technically intact (50d > 200d, price above both), but the path of least resistance is now lower toward the 23.6% Fib (7,330) and primary re-entry at the 38.2% Fib (7,151).
trend_regime: Golden Cross intact: 50-day above 200-day, price still above both. +7.0% premium to 50-day SMA (rapidly compressed from +10.6% on Jun 2) and +11.5% premium to 200-day SMA. VIX at the 20 threshold makes moving averages explicitly less reliable.
valuation_bucket: 20–22x (Cautious) — Forward P/E 21.11x (improved from 21.75x on the selloff); ERP +0.237%, still deeply inadequate (caution zone, below 1%).
what_changed_today: SPX fell −200.57 pts (−2.64%) to 7,383.74 — the largest single-day loss since the April tariff shock, vs a quiet −25 pt drift on June 4.
Relentless open-to-close distribution: high was the open (7,541.81), close near LOD (7,368.63), no intraday bounce — textbook institutional selling.
Caution Zone (−3% from ATH, 7,392) breached on a closing basis for the first time; intraday low (7,368) briefly violated the 23.6% Fib at 7,330.
VIX spiked +5 pts from ~15 to ~20 — now AT the methodology's elevated-risk threshold (was complacent ~15 for prior four sessions).
F&G collapsed 13 points in one session (55 → 42, Fear) — the fastest single-session deterioration of the cycle; Safe Haven downgraded two tiers to Fear (bonds outperforming).
Monte Carlo INVERTED: P(Wave 1 7,696 first vs 38.2% Fib 7,151) fell from 77.3% to 47.8% — model now favors downside first; edge below the 65% threshold.
Action shifted from 'hold + execute Wave 1 trim on touch / GTC live' to 'cancel Wave 1 GTC limit, hold, activate defensive tripwires' — Wave 1 at 7,696 was never executed.
50-day SMA premium compressed sharply from +10.6% (Jun 2) to +7.0%; 6-month multi-month bearish RSI divergence has now triggered.
narrative_summary: The ATH reversal is now confirmed: SPX fell −2.64% to 7,383.74 in a relentless open-to-close distribution day, the largest loss since the April shock, completing a clean three-session top from the 7,620 ATH. Every cross-layer warning the framework flagged over prior sessions has now fired — F&G collapsed 13 points to Fear, Safe Haven flipped to Fear as bonds outperform, VIX spiked to the 20 risk threshold, and the Monte Carlo first-hit bias inverted to favor downside (47.8% upside / 51.9% downside), breaching the 65% action threshold. Because Wave 1 (7,696) was never executed, the position is holding full SCHK exposure into a confirmed correction, so the disciplined stance is to cancel the now-stale Wave 1 GTC limit, hold the core position (Golden Cross intact), and shift to capital-preservation tripwires — a 10–15% defensive trim only on a close below 7,330, with the 38.2% Fib at 7,151 set as the primary re-entry zone. This is not yet a buying opportunity; discipline means waiting for the Fib support benchmarks and a clean confirmation close back above 7,550 rather than chasing.
open_questions: Does the 23.6% Fib at 7,330 hold on a closing basis, or does a weekend macro gap-down breach it and open the path to 7,151?
Will Put/Call (still ~0.60, complacent) finally expand into hedging — a sign the correction has more room — or compress as fear peaks?
Does junk-bond Extreme Fear (4th day at April-shock spreads) resolve via a VIX spike to meet credit, or does credit compress to meet equity?
Will VIX push decisively above 20–22 (deeper elevated/crisis risk) or fall back through 22, which would be a powerful re-entry trigger?
Is the multi-month bearish RSI divergence signaling a shallow 23.6% pullback or a deeper move toward the 50% Fib at 7,005?

decision_matrix.valuation: Forward P/E 21.11x (Cautious 20–22x bucket); ERP +0.237% — improved on selloff but still well below 1% (thin/ceiling).
decision_matrix.technicals: Confirmed distribution: close near LOD, Caution Zone (7,392) breached, 23.6% Fib (7,330) only 53 pts below; RSI ~65 breaking down, MFI rollover, multi-month bearish RSI divergence triggered.
decision_matrix.sentiment: F&G 42 (Fear, −13 in one session); Safe Haven flipped to Fear; Put/Call still complacent ~0.60; VIX ~20 — elevated-risk flag active, MAs less reliable.
decision_matrix.risk: Elevated — VIX at threshold, forced-selling risk rising, junk-bond Extreme Fear persistent (4th day); liquidation zones: −10% margin wave 6,859, −15% cascade 6,478.

confirming_evidence: Relentless open-to-close distribution day closing near LOD (7,368) confirms institutional selling — no capitulation spike, no reversal.
Caution Zone at 7,392 (−3% from ATH) breached on a closing basis for the first time this cycle.
F&G collapsed 13 points to 42 (Fear) and Safe Haven downgraded two tiers to Fear — active capital rotation out of equities.
VIX spiked to ~20, meeting the methodology's elevated-risk flag; moving averages explicitly less reliable.
Monte Carlo first-hit bias inverted to favor downside (47.8% up / 51.9% down) and edge is below 65% — no bullish action supported.
Multi-month (6-month) bearish RSI divergence triggered alongside a decisive 1-month MFI rollover — institutional money flow reversing.
Junk-bond Extreme Fear persisting at April-shock spread levels for a 4th day — credit stress not abating.

conflicting_evidence.bullish_read: Golden Cross remains intact — 50d above 200d and price still +7.0% above the 50-day and +11.5% above the 200-day; regime has not broken.
conflicting_evidence.bearish_read: Price is in a confirmed three-session distribution top, closed at the LOD, breached the Caution Zone, and is only 53 pts above the 23.6% Fib — the regime is intact only by lagging-MA construction.
conflicting_evidence.framework_rule: VIX > 20 = moving averages less reliable; mixed/ambiguous regime-vs-action data means hold and monitor, do not force a trade.

conflicting_evidence.bullish_read: Put/Call still ~0.60 (Extreme Greed) — no panic hedging yet; not a washed-out, contrarian buyable bottom.
conflicting_evidence.bearish_read: Same complacency means bears are not yet hedging, so the correction likely deepens before a tradable low forms; F&G (42) and Safe Haven (Fear) have already flipped while Put/Call lags.
conflicting_evidence.framework_rule: Sentiment must be confirmed by technical levels; never act on sentiment alone — falling P/C complacency into a breakdown argues for patience, not deployment.

conflicting_evidence.bullish_read: Selloff improved ERP from +0.097% to +0.237% and Forward P/E from 21.75x to 21.11x — valuation is getting cheaper.
conflicting_evidence.bearish_read: ERP +0.237% is still deeply inadequate; a 2% fair-value ERP implies ~6,360 (~14% lower), so fundamentals provide no near-term floor.
conflicting_evidence.framework_rule: ERP below 1% = caution zone, reduce aggression; do not add aggressively on thin risk premium.

conflicting_evidence.bullish_read: P(38.2% Fib 7,151 never hit in 60d) ~35% and P(50% Fib never hit) ~51% — meaningful chance the deeper re-entry zones are never reached, cost of waiting in cash.
conflicting_evidence.bearish_read: P(23.6% Fib 7,330 hit) 85.7% and P(38.2% Fib 7,151 hit) 64.7% — downside levels are now more likely than not, and the primary first-hit contest inverted below 50%.
conflicting_evidence.framework_rule: Monte Carlo edge < 65% = insufficient; flag monitor-only / no action — neither aggressive buying nor trimming is supported.
chart_refs: Step 5 Monte Carlo first-hit and conditional table

primary_tension: A still-technically-intact Golden Cross regime (50d > 200d, price +7% above the 50-day) and an improving-but-thin valuation versus a fully-confirmed institutional distribution correction in which every sentiment and probability layer has flipped bearish in a single session — F&G crashing to Fear, Safe Haven rotating to bonds, VIX hitting the 20 risk threshold, junk-bond Extreme Fear persisting, and the Monte Carlo first-hit bias inverting below the 65% action threshold. With Wave 1 never executed, the resolution is capital preservation: hold full core, cancel the stale upside limit, and wait for Fib support to define re-entry rather than force any trade.

monte_carlo.conditional_cascade: P(23.6% Fib 7,330 hit within 60d) 85.7% (near-certain); given that, P(38.2% Fib 7,151 hit within 60d) 64.7%; P(50.0% Fib 7,005 hit within 60d) 49.2% (coin-flip). Near-term P(7,500 first vs 7,300) 46.8%/53.2% — bias down.
monte_carlo.median_days: 23.6% Fib (7,330) within days on a single red open (~0.7% away); 38.2% Fib (7,151) over ~3–10 sessions per scenario table
